text:
Abstract Purpose: This study aims to identify the interpretative framework for the analysis of competitiveness of tourism destinations characterized by a high level of cultural capital composed of tangible and intangible assets. Special attention is devoted to those contexts where cultural and creative industries form the basis of the new development strategies for the economy of the territory. Methods: The study carried out a critical review of literature on tourism destinations and cultural capitals, focusing on the economic role of cultural and creative industries at the territorial level, starting from both national and international literature. Findings: The creative industries are increasingly considered tools for revitalizing the economy of the territory. Originality/value: This paper is a first attempt in the scientific field to identify the interpretative framework of the competitiveness of tourism destinations with a high cultural content.

This research aims to establish a model to serve as a theoretical reference for designing the integrated and overall management policies for creative tourism destinations. Currently, the research on destination management is quite mature and there is a lack of integrated research on the relationship between cultural heritage, creative industry and local development, which is the starting point and deficiency of this study.

Even if the methodology is based on a progressive approach to existing knowledge, it introduces an extremely innovative perspective in that it bridges the gap between conservation-oriented approaches to heritage management and the needs of the creative economy. Our research contributes to the on-going scientific debate on the subject while at the same time also providing an operative tool for regional policy makers. A tool that can be effectively used to implement strategic projects for increasing territorial well-being through sustainable and creative tourism.
